center.name: 太平洋西北地区
center.summary: 太平洋西北地区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括Cocos booby、庫柏鷹、Chestnut-backed chickadee，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|left|An illustration of two chestnut-backed chickadees by Louis Agassiz Fuertes It is found in the Pacific Northwest of the United States and western Canada, from southeastern Alaska to southwestern California

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In recent years, Cocos booby has dispersed and is now found in the Central and West Pacific in Japan and Hawaii where they have now bred. They are only recent resident breeders in California, first breeding in the Channel Islands in 2017, and now regularly occur as far north on the Pacific coast as Vancouver. Large colonies exist on the islands of Clipperton Island, San Pedro Mártir Island, Isla Espíritu Santo, Isla Isabel, Islas Marietas, Socorro Island, Isla del Caño, Gorgona Island, and Cocos Island

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They tend to be most migratory in the north and largely to partially sedentary elsewhere. With individual exceptions, hawks of the species largely migrate out of nearly all of their range in southern Canada as well as cooler parts of the Pacific Northwest, essentially all of Montana and northern parts of surrounding states, the Dakotas (but for southern South Dakota), the northern parts of the Great Lake states, northern New York and much of New England. Despite being classed as sedentary, banding studies have revealed that many Cooper's hawks south of the typical migratory populations engage in some variety of seasonal winter movements.Goodrich, L
